inputs.problem_text:
From January to April 2017, monthly average % non FPQ of filtration process by batch (per discharge) was 18 % 
Note: % non FPQ  filtration process  means number of  %  batch offspec acidity  in filter cake out put per month
inputs.goal_text: Reduce  % non FPQ  on Filtration  Process by batch to 8% (tbc) by end of December 2017.
voc_list: filter cake output maintain the acidity contain per product specification

outputs.problem_statement: From January to April 2017, the monthly average percentage of non-FPQ (First Pass Quality) batches in the filtration process was 18%. This indicates a significant portion of batches did not meet the acidity specifications in the filter cake output.
outputs.goal_statement: Reduce the percentage of non-FPQ batches in the Filtration Process to 8% by the end of December 2017.
outputs.business_case: Improving the quality of the filter cake output by reducing off-spec batches will enhance product quality, reduce waste, and improve customer satisfaction, potentially leading to increased market share and profitability.

in_scope: Filtration process within the production area
Calibration and maintenance of filter equipment
Filtration process parameters and standard operating procedures
Quality of raw materials and chemical additives
Accuracy of acidity measurements and calibration of pH meters
Environmental factors such as ambient temperature and humidity levels
out_of_scope: Upstream processes such as raw material sourcing
Downstream processes such as packaging and distribution

ctq_list.name: Percentage of Off-Spec Batches
ctq_list.description: The percentage of batches that do not meet the acidity specifications in the filter cake output.

ctq_list.name: Acidity Level in Filter Cake
ctq_list.description: The acidity level of the filter cake output must meet product specifications to ensure quality.

measures: Monitor the percentage of non-FPQ batches monthly
Track acidity levels in filter cake outputs bi-weekly
Conduct regular calibration checks on pH meters

define_state.insight_md:
- The selected CTQ, "Percentage of Off-Spec Batches," directly addresses the key issue of maintaining acidity levels in filter cake output, aligning with the primary pain theme of quality.
- The scope is well-defined to focus on the filtration process, ensuring targeted improvements without overextending resources into unrelated areas.
- Key risks include potential inaccuracies in acidity measurement and variability in raw material quality, which could affect the project's success.
- Assumptions include consistent environmental conditions and operator adherence to standard operating procedures.
- Past successful projects in manufacturing quality improvement suggest focusing on equipment calibration and operator training as critical factors.
- Similar episodes highlight the importance of defining clear measurement systems and regularly reviewing process parameters to ensure consistent quality.
- Next steps for Measure: 
  - Develop a detailed measurement plan to track the percentage of non-FPQ batches and acidity levels in filter cake outputs.
  - Implement a system for regular calibration checks of pH meters and other measurement equipment.
  - Conduct an initial baseline measurement to validate current process performance and identify any immediate discrepancies.

primary_ctq.name: Percentage of Off-Spec Batches
primary_ctq.description: The percentage of batches that do not meet the acidity specifications in the filter cake output.
